prompt: The toString() method doesn't produce the expected output. What is the bug?
choices: toString() cannot be called explicitly; it must only be invoked implicitly
Calling q.toString() on a null reference throws NullPointerException; string concatenation 'q=' + q would safely produce 'q=null' instead
The return statement in toString() should use StringBuilder
System.out.println() does not call toString() automatically
explanation: q is null. q.toString() dereferences the null reference → NullPointerException. String concatenation ('q=' + q) would work safely because Java checks for null before calling toString(). Fix: use 'q=' + q instead of q.toString().
